title: oenothera elata subsp. elata
intro: Oenothera elata subsp. elata, also known as Evening Primrose, is a flowering plant species native to western North America, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and yellow anthers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its rich source of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated fatty acid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oenothera elata subsp. elata include reducing symptoms of menopause, alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and supporting heart health due to its ability to lower cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including capsules, tinctures, and oil extracts, which can be used to support overall well-being and health.
